title: Model experiments became an architectural stress test
excerpt: I've been tuning Codenames AI, a small web game where an LLM plays Codenames with you. Clue...
content_html:
I've been tuning Codenames AI, a small web game where an LLM plays Codenames with you. Clue generation is tightly constrained: one word, a count, optional intended targets, JSON on the wire, then deterministic validation before anything reaches the board.

As the project started attracting regular players, I wanted to improve the gameplay experience without blowing out costs. Moving one model generation from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-5-mini was my first instinct.

The default reasoning setting made responses an order of magnitude slower for this workload. Minimal reasoning looked like the obvious compromise: newer model, responsive gameplay.

I expected to compare clue quality, latency, and cost while the surrounding prompt, validator, and consumer contracts stayed put.

That last part was wrong.

The experiment stopped behaving like an A/B test

What showed up was structural, and it showed up in places that had been stable for months.

Validation failures started rising. Retries started rising. Entire candidate batches started failing before the game ever saw a clue. The sharpest signal came from a clue-selection path that had run untouched for months, and it hard-failed for the first time. They weren't latency regressions so much as architectural ones.

It is easy to read that as "minimal reasoning made the model worse." More often, the failures were exposing gaps in contracts that had looked fine under the previous model.

What each failure actually invalidated

Eventually every failure traced back to one of three layers:

  1. Prompt contracts ask for exactly count targets and, in batch mode, several distinct candidates.
  2. Deterministic validators reject target/count mismatches and filter invalid candidates before anything downstream runs.
  3. Downstream consumers only see survivors. Empty batches retry with rejection feedback, then fall back if needed.

Those layers share one job: enforce the same invariants. The failures below cut across all three rather than mapping one to one.

Side commentary could kill an otherwise usable turn.

To pick a clue, one strategy (Strange mode) simulates how the AI guesser would respond to each candidate clue, then scores those simulated turns and keeps the best one. I thought those simulations would fail only when the guesses themselves were bad. After the swap, they could also fail because the model attached commentary about other words it had considered, including words that were not even on the board. Because the payload schema included that commentary, the validator had to treat it as part of the same all-or-nothing contract. A payload with usable guesses still got rejected, and when every candidate died that way, the turn came back as a controlled API failure instead of a clue.

Target cardinality had to match the clue count.

I thought my validator was protecting the game. Instead I discovered the previous model had been consistently producing outputs that satisfied those contracts.

Say the prompt asks for count: 2 and a targets array with exactly two unrevealed friendly codenames. Under the old model, a clue like {"word": "BUILDING", "count": 2, "targets": ["TOWER", "CASTLE"]} usually meant two real board words. After the swap, I started seeing the same shape with one valid target and one word that is not on the grid at all, or only a single target when count was 2. Valid JSON. Perfect keys. Intent status: invalid.

The validator rejects clues whose validated targets don't match count. Valid JSON wasn't enough.

Retries assumed the contracts were already specific enough.

I thought retries were simply robustness. Instead they became diagnostic tooling because they finally told me which invariant had actually failed. When a batch fails validation, the retry path can attach rejection feedback (failed clue words plus reason strings) so the next attempt is not a blind redo. That only helps if the contracts are specific enough to name the failure. Vague "try again" prompts hide whether you have a model problem or an underspecified invariant.

The failures showed up in the product, not just the logs.

Every rejected clue meant another retry before the player saw a move. On an AI spymaster turn, the game shows a clue, a count, and highlights the board words that clue is meant to cover. When the validated targets came back shorter than count, the UI looked broken: count: 2 with only one word highlighted. The AI guesser still trusted the clue count and started reasoning from a board state that never actually existed.

None of this required a different product thesis from schema-first validation. Valid JSON was never enough. The migration stress-tested whether prompt text, deterministic checks, and consumer assumptions still agreed after the model changed.

The uncomfortable part

On paper, the clue path already looked responsible. Prompt, validator, consumer. Clean separation.

The migration revealed a hidden layer:

Prompt
  ↓
Model capability
  (compensating for weak contracts)
  ↓
Validator
  ↓
Consumer

I expected to compare models. Instead I ended up comparing how much of my architecture each model had been compensating for.

While a more capable model kept quietly covering those weak contracts, the dashboards looked fine. Drop reasoning effort, and the same prompts start producing outputs that are honest about what you actually specified. Once that stopped happening, I was no longer measuring model quality. I was measuring how much of the gameplay experience had been resting on those hidden assumptions.

That is uncomfortable and useful. Apparent regressions (count mismatches, partial batches, more retries, collapsed guess simulations) are a signal to ask which layer was doing the work: the model, or the application.

Subjective "does this clue feel clever?" still matters for gameplay. It should not be the only scoreboard when the pipeline can reject an entire batch before the server ever picks a clue.

Treat migrations as compatibility tests

What I want out of a model swap now:

  1. Align invariants across prompt, validator, and consumer. If the prompt says "exactly count targets," the validator must reject mismatches, and the API response shape must not pretend invalid intent is OK.
  2. Keep structural correctness in deterministic code. Use the model for association quality. Use pure functions for board membership, cardinality, illegal clue shapes, and survivor lists.
  3. Instrument validation failures by category. First-pass success rate, retry rate, and failure reasons tell you whether you tightened a contract or uncovered a real model gap.
  4. Evaluate end-to-end workflow metrics, not only single-call latency or token price. Retries and fallbacks change the bill and the player experience; measuring only the happy path lies.

Takeaway: A model migration tests the model and the architecture around it. If prompt, validator, and consumer contracts do not enforce the same invariants, stronger models can mask weaknesses in those contracts until a cheaper or more literal model exposes them. The lesson is not really about which LLM you pick. It is about architectural coupling: the model itself had become part of the contract without me noticing.

If you'd like to see the project that inspired these lessons, you can try Codenames AI.

blocks.html_en: I've been tuning Codenames AI, a small web game where an LLM plays Codenames with you. Clue generation is tightly constrained: one word, a count, optional intended targets, JSON on the wire, then deterministic validation before anything reaches the board.

sentences.en: I've been tuning Codenames AI, a small web game where an LLM plays Codenames with you.
sentences.zh: 我一直在调整 Codenames AI，这是一款小型网页游戏，法学硕士可以和你一起玩 Codenames。

sentences.en: Clue generation is tightly constrained: one word, a count, optional intended targets, JSON on the wire, then deterministic validation before anything reaches the board.
sentences.zh: 线索生成受到严格限制：一个单词、一个计数、可选的预期目标、线路上的 JSON，然后在任何内容到达主板之前进行确定性验证。

blocks.html_en: As the project started attracting regular players, I wanted to improve the gameplay experience without blowing out costs. Moving one model generation from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-5-mini was my first instinct.

sentences.en: As the project started attracting regular players, I wanted to improve the gameplay experience without blowing out costs.
sentences.zh: 随着该项目开始吸引普通玩家，我希望在不增加成本的情况下改善游戏体验。

sentences.en: Moving one model generation from gpt-4o-mini to gpt-5-mini was my first instinct.
sentences.zh: 将一代型号从 gpt-4o-mini 转移到 gpt-5-mini 是我的第一直觉。

blocks.html_en: The default reasoning setting made responses an order of magnitude slower for this workload. Minimal reasoning looked like the obvious compromise: newer model, responsive gameplay.

sentences.en: The default reasoning setting made responses an order of magnitude slower for this workload.
sentences.zh: 默认推理设置使该工作负载的响应速度慢了一个数量级。

sentences.en: Minimal reasoning looked like the obvious compromise: newer model, responsive gameplay.
sentences.zh: 最小推理看起来像是明显的妥协：更新的模型，响应式的游戏玩法。

blocks.html_en: I expected to compare clue quality, latency, and cost while the surrounding prompt, validator, and consumer contracts stayed put.

sentences.en: I expected to compare clue quality, latency, and cost while the surrounding prompt, validator, and consumer contracts stayed put.
sentences.zh: 我希望在周围的提示、验证器和消费者合约保持不变的情况下比较线索质量、延迟和成本。

blocks.html_en: That last part was wrong.

sentences.en: That last part was wrong.
sentences.zh: 最后一部分是错误的。

blocks.html_en: The experiment stopped behaving like an A/B test

sentences.en: The experiment stopped behaving like an A/B test
sentences.zh: 实验不再像 A/B 测试一样

blocks.html_en: What showed up was structural, and it showed up in places that had been stable for months.

sentences.en: What showed up was structural, and it showed up in places that had been stable for months.
sentences.zh: 出现的情况是结构性的，而且出现在几个月来一直稳定的地方。

blocks.html_en: Validation failures started rising. Retries started rising. Entire candidate batches started failing before the game ever saw a clue. The sharpest signal came from a clue-selection path that had run untouched for months, and it hard-failed for the first time. They weren't latency regressions so much as architectural ones.

sentences.en: Validation failures started rising.
sentences.zh: 验证失败开始增加。

sentences.en: Retries started rising.
sentences.zh: 重试次数开始上升。

sentences.en: Entire candidate batches started failing before the game ever saw a clue.
sentences.zh: 在游戏看到线索之前，整个候选批次就开始失败。

sentences.en: The sharpest signal came from a clue-selection path that had run untouched for months, and it hard-failed for the first time.
sentences.zh: 最尖锐的信号来自一条线索选择路径，该路径已连续运行数月未受影响，并且首次出现故障。

sentences.en: They weren't latency regressions so much as architectural ones.
sentences.zh: 与其说它们是延迟回归，不如说是架构回归。

blocks.html_en: It is easy to read that as "minimal reasoning made the model worse." More often, the failures were exposing gaps in contracts that had looked fine under the previous model.

sentences.en: It is easy to read that as "minimal reasoning made the model worse."
sentences.zh: 很容易理解为“最少的推理使模型变得更糟”。

sentences.en: More often, the failures were exposing gaps in contracts that had looked fine under the previous model.
sentences.zh: 更常见的是，这些失败暴露了合同中的漏洞，而在之前的模式下，这些合同看起来还不错。

blocks.html_en: What each failure actually invalidated

sentences.en: What each failure actually invalidated
sentences.zh: 每次失败实际上使什么失效

blocks.html_en: Eventually every failure traced back to one of three layers:

sentences.en: Eventually every failure traced back to one of three layers:
sentences.zh: 最终，每个故障都可以追溯到以下三层之一：

blocks.html_en: • Prompt contracts ask for exactly count targets and, in batch mode, several distinct candidates.

sentences.en: Prompt contracts ask for exactly count targets and, in batch mode, several distinct candidates.
sentences.zh: 即时合同要求精确的计数目标，并在批处理模式下要求几个不同的候选者。

blocks.html_en: • Deterministic validators reject target/count mismatches and filter invalid candidates before anything downstream runs.

sentences.en: Deterministic validators reject target/count mismatches and filter invalid candidates before anything downstream runs.
sentences.zh: 确定性验证器会拒绝目标/计数不匹配，并在下游运行之前过滤无效候选者。

blocks.html_en: • Downstream consumers only see survivors. Empty batches retry with rejection feedback, then fall back if needed.

sentences.en: Downstream consumers only see survivors.
sentences.zh: 下游消费者只看到幸存者。

sentences.en: Empty batches retry with rejection feedback, then fall back if needed.
sentences.zh: 空批次重试并提供拒绝反馈，然后在需要时回退。

sentences.en: Those layers share one job: enforce the same invariants.
sentences.zh: 这些层共享一项工作：强制执行相同的不变量。

sentences.en: The failures below cut across all three rather than mapping one to one.
sentences.zh: 下面的失败涵盖了所有三个，而不是一一对应。

sentences.en: Side commentary could kill an otherwise usable turn.
sentences.zh: 旁注可能会扼杀原本可用的回合。

After the swap, they could also fail because the model attached commentary about other words it had considered, including words that were not even on the board. Because the payload schema included that commentary, the validator had to treat it as part of the same all-or-nothing contract. A payload with usable guesses still got rejected, and when every candidate died that way, the turn came back as a controlled API failure instead of a clue.

sentences.en: To pick a clue, one strategy (Strange mode) simulates how the AI guesser would respond to each candidate clue, then scores those simulated turns and keeps the best one.
sentences.zh: 为了选择线索，一种策略（奇怪模式）会模拟人工智能猜测者如何响应每个候选线索，然后对这些模拟回合进行评分并保留最好的一个。

sentences.en: I thought those simulations would fail only when the guesses themselves were bad.
sentences.zh: 我认为只有当猜测本身很糟糕时，这些模拟才会失败。

sentences.en: After the swap, they could also fail because the model attached commentary about other words it had considered, including words that were not even on the board.
sentences.zh: 交换后，它们也可能会失败，因为模型附加了对其考虑过的其他单词的评论，包括甚至不在黑板上的单词。

sentences.en: Because the payload schema included that commentary, the validator had to treat it as part of the same all-or-nothing contract.
sentences.zh: 由于有效负载模式包含该注释，因此验证器必须将其视为同一个“全有或全无”合同的一部分。

sentences.en: A payload with usable guesses still got rejected, and when every candidate died that way, the turn came back as a controlled API failure instead of a clue.
sentences.zh: 具有可用猜测的有效负载仍然被拒绝，当每个候选者都以这种方式死亡时，轮回作为受控 API 故障而不是线索返回。

blocks.html_en: Target cardinality had to match the clue count.

sentences.en: Target cardinality had to match the clue count.
sentences.zh: 目标基数必须与线索计数相匹配。

blocks.html_en: I thought my validator was protecting the game. Instead I discovered the previous model had been consistently producing outputs that satisfied those contracts.

sentences.en: I thought my validator was protecting the game.
sentences.zh: 我以为我的验证器正在保护游戏。

sentences.en: Instead I discovered the previous model had been consistently producing outputs that satisfied those contracts.
sentences.zh: 相反，我发现以前的模型一直在生产满足这些合同的输出。

After the swap, I started seeing the same shape with one valid target and one word that is not on the grid at all, or only a single target when count was 2. Valid JSON. Perfect keys. Intent status: invalid.

sentences.en: Say the prompt asks for count: 2 and a targets array with exactly two unrevealed friendly codenames.
sentences.zh: 假设提示要求 count: 2 和一个包含两个未公开的友好代号的目标数组。

sentences.en: Under the old model, a clue like {"word": "BUILDING", "count": 2, "targets": ["TOWER", "CASTLE"]} usually meant two real board words.
sentences.zh: 在旧模型下，像 {"word": "BUILDING", "count": 2, "targets": ["TOWER", "CASTLE"]} 这样的线索通常意味着两个真实的棋盘单词。

sentences.en: After the swap, I started seeing the same shape with one valid target and one word that is not on the grid at all, or only a single target when count was 2.
sentences.zh: 交换后，我开始看到相同的形状，其中有一个有效目标和一个根本不在网格上的单词，或者当计数为 2 时只有一个目标。

sentences.en: Valid JSON.
sentences.zh: 有效的 JSON。

sentences.en: Perfect keys.
sentences.zh: 完美的钥匙。

sentences.en: Intent status: invalid.
sentences.zh: 意向状态：无效。

sentences.zh: 我认为重试只是鲁棒性。

sentences.zh: 相反，它们成为了诊断工具，因为它们最终告诉我哪个不变量实际上失败了。

sentences.en: When a batch fails validation, the retry path can attach rejection feedback (failed clue words plus reason strings) so the next attempt is not a blind redo.
sentences.zh: 当批次验证失败时，重试路径可以附加拒绝反馈（失败的线索词加上原因字符串），因此下一次尝试不是盲目重做。

sentences.en: That only helps if the contracts are specific enough to name the failure.
sentences.zh: 只有当合同足够具体以指出失败时，这才有帮助。

sentences.zh: 模糊的“重试”提示会隐藏您是否存在模型问题或未指定的不变量。

sentences.en: On an AI spymaster turn, the game shows a clue, a count, and highlights the board words that clue is meant to cover.
sentences.zh: 在人工智能间谍大师回合中，游戏会显示一条线索、一个计数，并突出显示该线索要涵盖的棋盘单词。

sentences.en: When the validated targets came back shorter than count, the UI looked broken: count: 2 with only one word highlighted.
sentences.zh: 当验证的目标返回的时间短于 count 时，UI 看起来很损坏：count: 2，仅突出显示一个单词。

sentences.en: The AI guesser still trusted the clue count and started reasoning from a board state that never actually existed.
sentences.zh: 人工智能猜测者仍然相信线索计数，并从实际上从未存在过的棋盘状态开始推理。

blocks.html_en: None of this required a different product thesis from schema-first validation. Valid JSON was never enough. The migration stress-tested whether prompt text, deterministic checks, and consumer assumptions still agreed after the model changed.

sentences.zh: 有效的 JSON 永远不够。

sentences.en: The migration stress-tested whether prompt text, deterministic checks, and consumer assumptions still agreed after the model changed.

sentences.zh: 不舒服的部分

blocks.html_en: On paper, the clue path already looked responsible. Prompt, validator, consumer. Clean separation.

sentences.en: On paper, the clue path already looked responsible.
sentences.zh: 从纸面上看，线索路径看起来已经是负责任的了。

sentences.en: Prompt, validator, consumer.
sentences.zh: 提示者、验证者、消费者。

sentences.en: Clean separation.
sentences.zh: 干净的分离。

blocks.html_en: The migration revealed a hidden layer:

sentences.en: The migration revealed a hidden layer:
sentences.zh: 迁移揭示了一个隐藏层：

sentences.en: Enter fullscreen mode Exit fullscreen mode
sentences.zh: 进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

blocks.html_en: I expected to compare models. Instead I ended up comparing how much of my architecture each model had been compensating for.

sentences.en: I expected to compare models.
sentences.zh: 我期望比较模型。

sentences.en: Instead I ended up comparing how much of my architecture each model had been compensating for.
sentences.zh: 相反，我最终比较了每个模型补偿了我的架构的多少。

blocks.html_en: While a more capable model kept quietly covering those weak contracts, the dashboards looked fine. Drop reasoning effort, and the same prompts start producing outputs that are honest about what you actually specified. Once that stopped happening, I was no longer measuring model quality. I was measuring how much of the gameplay experience had been resting on those hidden assumptions.

sentences.en: While a more capable model kept quietly covering those weak contracts, the dashboards looked fine.
sentences.zh: 虽然功能更强大的模型一直在悄悄地覆盖那些薄弱的合约，但仪表板看起来还不错。

sentences.en: Drop reasoning effort, and the same prompts start producing outputs that are honest about what you actually specified.
sentences.zh: 放弃推理工作，相同的提示开始产生与您实际指定的内容相符的输出。

sentences.en: Once that stopped happening, I was no longer measuring model quality.
sentences.zh: 一旦这种情况停止发生，我就不再衡量模型质量。

sentences.en: I was measuring how much of the gameplay experience had been resting on those hidden assumptions.
sentences.zh: 我正在衡量有多少游戏体验是基于这些隐藏的假设。

blocks.html_en: That is uncomfortable and useful. Apparent regressions (count mismatches, partial batches, more retries, collapsed guess simulations) are a signal to ask which layer was doing the work: the model, or the application.

sentences.en: That is uncomfortable and useful.
sentences.zh: 这既不舒服又有用。

sentences.en: Apparent regressions (count mismatches, partial batches, more retries, collapsed guess simulations) are a signal to ask which layer was doing the work: the model, or the application.
sentences.zh: 明显的回归（计数不匹配、部分批次、更多重试、崩溃的猜测模拟）是询问哪一层正在执行工作的信号：模型还是应用程序。

blocks.html_en: Subjective "does this clue feel clever?" still matters for gameplay. It should not be the only scoreboard when the pipeline can reject an entire batch before the server ever picks a clue.

sentences.en: Subjective "does this clue feel clever?"
sentences.zh: 主观“这条线索感觉聪明吗？”

sentences.en: still matters for gameplay.
sentences.zh: 对于游戏玩法来说仍然很重要。

sentences.en: It should not be the only scoreboard when the pipeline can reject an entire batch before the server ever picks a clue.
sentences.zh: 当管道在服务器选择线索之前拒绝整个批次时，它不应该是唯一的记分板。

blocks.html_en: Treat migrations as compatibility tests

sentences.en: Treat migrations as compatibility tests
sentences.zh: 将迁移视为兼容性测试

blocks.html_en: What I want out of a model swap now:

sentences.en: What I want out of a model swap now:
sentences.zh: 我现在想要从模型交换中得到什么：

blocks.html_en: • Align invariants across prompt, validator, and consumer. If the prompt says "exactly count targets," the validator must reject mismatches, and the API response shape must not pretend invalid intent is OK.

sentences.en: Align invariants across prompt, validator, and consumer.
sentences.zh: 跨提示、验证器和消费者对齐不变量。

sentences.en: If the prompt says "exactly count targets," the validator must reject mismatches, and the API response shape must not pretend invalid intent is OK.
sentences.zh: 如果提示显示“精确计算目标”，则验证器必须拒绝不匹配，并且 API 响应形状不得假装无效意图正常。

blocks.html_en: • Keep structural correctness in deterministic code. Use the model for association quality. Use pure functions for board membership, cardinality, illegal clue shapes, and survivor lists.

sentences.en: Keep structural correctness in deterministic code.
sentences.zh: 保持确定性代码的结构正确性。

sentences.en: Use the model for association quality.
sentences.zh: 使用关联质量模型。

sentences.en: Use pure functions for board membership, cardinality, illegal clue shapes, and survivor lists.
sentences.zh: 使用纯函数来处理董事会成员资格、基数、非法线索形状和幸存者列表。

blocks.html_en: • Instrument validation failures by category. First-pass success rate, retry rate, and failure reasons tell you whether you tightened a contract or uncovered a real model gap.

sentences.en: Instrument validation failures by category.
sentences.zh: 按类别划分的仪器验证失败。

sentences.en: First-pass success rate, retry rate, and failure reasons tell you whether you tightened a contract or uncovered a real model gap.
sentences.zh: 首次通过成功率、重试率和失败原因告诉您是否收紧了合同或发现了真正的模型差距。

blocks.html_en: • Evaluate end-to-end workflow metrics, not only single-call latency or token price. Retries and fallbacks change the bill and the player experience; measuring only the happy path lies.

sentences.en: Evaluate end-to-end workflow metrics, not only single-call latency or token price.
sentences.zh: 评估端到端工作流程指标，而不仅仅是单次调用延迟或代币价格。

sentences.en: Retries and fallbacks change the bill and the player experience; measuring only the happy path lies.
sentences.zh: 重试和回退会改变账单和玩家体验；只衡量幸福之路所在。

blocks.html_en: Takeaway: A model migration tests the model and the architecture around it. If prompt, validator, and consumer contracts do not enforce the same invariants, stronger models can mask weaknesses in those contracts until a cheaper or more literal model exposes them. The lesson is not really about which LLM you pick. It is about architectural coupling: the model itself had become part of the contract without me noticing.

sentences.en: Takeaway: A model migration tests the model and the architecture around it.
sentences.zh: 要点：模型迁移测试模型及其周围的架构。

sentences.en: If prompt, validator, and consumer contracts do not enforce the same invariants, stronger models can mask weaknesses in those contracts until a cheaper or more literal model exposes them.
sentences.zh: 如果提示器、验证器和消费者合约不强制执行相同的不变量，则更强的模型可以掩盖这些合约中的弱点，直到更便宜或更直接的模型暴露它们。

sentences.en: The lesson is not really about which LLM you pick.
sentences.zh: 该课程实际上并不是关于您选择哪个法学硕士。

sentences.en: It is about architectural coupling: the model itself had become part of the contract without me noticing.
sentences.zh: 这是关于架构耦合的：在我没有注意到的情况下，模型本身已经成为合同的一部分。

blocks.html_en: If you'd like to see the project that inspired these lessons, you can try Codenames AI.

sentences.en: If you'd like to see the project that inspired these lessons, you can try Codenames AI.
sentences.zh: 如果您想查看启发这些课程的项目，您可以尝试 Codenames AI。

content_html_zh:
我一直在调整 Codenames AI，这是一款小型网页游戏，法学硕士可以和你一起玩 Codenames。 线索生成受到严格限制：一个单词、一个计数、可选的预期目标、线路上的 JSON，然后在任何内容到达主板之前进行确定性验证。

随着该项目开始吸引普通玩家，我希望在不增加成本的情况下改善游戏体验。 将一代型号从 gpt-4o-mini 转移到 gpt-5-mini 是我的第一直觉。

默认推理设置使该工作负载的响应速度慢了一个数量级。 最小推理看起来像是明显的妥协：更新的模型，响应式的游戏玩法。

我希望在周围的提示、验证器和消费者合约保持不变的情况下比较线索质量、延迟和成本。

最后一部分是错误的。

实验不再像 A/B 测试一样

出现的情况是结构性的，而且出现在几个月来一直稳定的地方。

验证失败开始增加。 重试次数开始上升。 在游戏看到线索之前，整个候选批次就开始失败。 最尖锐的信号来自一条线索选择路径，该路径已连续运行数月未受影响，并且首次出现故障。 与其说它们是延迟回归，不如说是架构回归。

很容易理解为“最少的推理使模型变得更糟”。 更常见的是，这些失败暴露了合同中的漏洞，而在之前的模式下，这些合同看起来还不错。

每次失败实际上使什么失效

最终，每个故障都可以追溯到以下三层之一：

  1. 即时合同要求精确的计数目标，并在批处理模式下要求几个不同的候选者。
  2. 确定性验证器会拒绝目标/计数不匹配，并在下游运行之前过滤无效候选者。
  3. 下游消费者只看到幸存者。 空批次重试并提供拒绝反馈，然后在需要时回退。

这些层共享一项工作：强制执行相同的不变量。 下面的失败涵盖了所有三个，而不是一一对应。

旁注可能会扼杀原本可用的回合。

为了选择线索，一种策略（奇怪模式）会模拟人工智能猜测者如何响应每个候选线索，然后对这些模拟回合进行评分并保留最好的一个。 我认为只有当猜测本身很糟糕时，这些模拟才会失败。 交换后，它们也可能会失败，因为模型附加了对其考虑过的其他单词的评论，包括甚至不在黑板上的单词。 由于有效负载模式包含该注释，因此验证器必须将其视为同一个“全有或全无”合同的一部分。 具有可用猜测的有效负载仍然被拒绝，当每个候选者都以这种方式死亡时，轮回作为受控 API 故障而不是线索返回。

目标基数必须与线索计数相匹配。

我以为我的验证器正在保护游戏。 相反，我发现以前的模型一直在生产满足这些合同的输出。

假设提示要求 count: 2 和一个包含两个未公开的友好代号的目标数组。 在旧模型下，像 {"word": "BUILDING", "count": 2, "targets": ["TOWER", "CASTLE"]} 这样的线索通常意味着两个真实的棋盘单词。 交换后，我开始看到相同的形状，其中有一个有效目标和一个根本不在网格上的单词，或者当计数为 2 时只有一个目标。 有效的 JSON。 完美的钥匙。 意向状态：无效。

验证器会拒绝已验证目标与计数不匹配的线索。 有效的 JSON 还不够。

重试假设合同已经足够具体。

我认为重试只是鲁棒性。 相反，它们成为了诊断工具，因为它们最终告诉我哪个不变量实际上失败了。 当批次验证失败时，重试路径可以附加拒绝反馈（失败的线索词加上原因字符串），因此下一次尝试不是盲目重做。 只有当合同足够具体以指出失败时，这才有帮助。 模糊的“重试”提示会隐藏您是否存在模型问题或未指定的不变量。

故障显示在产品中，而不仅仅是日志中。

每一条被拒绝的线索都意味着玩家在看到行动之前要再次重试。 在人工智能间谍大师回合中，游戏会显示一条线索、一个计数，并突出显示该线索要涵盖的棋盘单词。 当验证的目标返回的时间短于 count 时，UI 看起来很损坏：count: 2，仅突出显示一个单词。 人工智能猜测者仍然相信线索计数，并从实际上从未存在过的棋盘状态开始推理。

所有这些都不需要与模式优先验证不同的产品论文。 有效的 JSON 永远不够。 迁移压力测试了模型更改后提示文本、确定性检查和消费者假设是否仍然一致。

不舒服的部分

从纸面上看，线索路径看起来已经是负责任的了。 提示者、验证者、消费者。 干净的分离。

迁移揭示了一个隐藏层：

Prompt
  ↓
Model capability
  (compensating for weak contracts)
  ↓
Validator
  ↓
Consumer

进入全屏模式 退出全屏模式

我期望比较模型。 相反，我最终比较了每个模型补偿了我的架构的多少。

虽然功能更强大的模型一直在悄悄地覆盖那些薄弱的合约，但仪表板看起来还不错。 放弃推理工作，相同的提示开始产生与您实际指定的内容相符的输出。 一旦这种情况停止发生，我就不再衡量模型质量。 我正在衡量有多少游戏体验是基于这些隐藏的假设。

这既不舒服又有用。 明显的回归（计数不匹配、部分批次、更多重试、崩溃的猜测模拟）是询问哪一层正在执行工作的信号：模型还是应用程序。

主观“这条线索感觉聪明吗？” 对于游戏玩法来说仍然很重要。 当管道在服务器选择线索之前拒绝整个批次时，它不应该是唯一的记分板。

将迁移视为兼容性测试

我现在想要从模型交换中得到什么：

  1. 跨提示、验证器和消费者对齐不变量。 如果提示显示“精确计算目标”，则验证器必须拒绝不匹配，并且 API 响应形状不得假装无效意图正常。
  2. 保持确定性代码的结构正确性。 使用关联质量模型。 使用纯函数来处理董事会成员资格、基数、非法线索形状和幸存者列表。
  3. 按类别划分的仪器验证失败。 首次通过成功率、重试率和失败原因告诉您是否收紧了合同或发现了真正的模型差距。
  4. 评估端到端工作流程指标，而不仅仅是单次调用延迟或代币价格。 重试和回退会改变账单和玩家体验；只衡量幸福之路所在。

要点：模型迁移测试模型及其周围的架构。 如果提示器、验证器和消费者合约不强制执行相同的不变量，则更强的模型可以掩盖这些合约中的弱点，直到更便宜或更直接的模型暴露它们。 该课程实际上并不是关于您选择哪个法学硕士。 这是关于架构耦合的：在我没有注意到的情况下，模型本身已经成为合同的一部分。

如果您想查看启发这些课程的项目，您可以尝试 Codenames AI。
